name: openEuler SIG Role Description | SIG Zone | openEuler
description: Learn about the responsibilities and work of each role in openEuler SIGs, and participate in community building.

mainEntity.name: What are the roles in openEuler SIG?

acceptedAnswer.text: The main roles in openEuler SIGs include: Maintainer (responsible for final code review and merging), Committer (has merge permissions for specific areas), and Contributor (participates in code contributions via PRs).

mainEntity.name: How to become a Maintainer in openEuler SIG?

acceptedAnswer.text: To become a Maintainer, you need to accumulate long-term high-quality contributions, be recommended by existing Maintainers, and be appointed after SIG discussion. Maintainers are responsible for code review, release management, and SIG direction decisions.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between Contributor and Committer?

acceptedAnswer.text: Contributors participate in code contributions by submitting Pull Requests but do not have merge permissions. Committers, after long-term contributions, gain merge permissions for specific modules and can review and merge other Contributors' PRs.
